message.abstract:
<jats:title>Summary</jats:title>
                <jats:p>The wave of COVID-19 continues to overwhelm the medical resources, especially the stressed intensive care unit (ICU) capacity and the shortage of mechanical ventilation (MV). Here we performed CT-based analysis combined with electronic health records and clinical laboratory results on Cohort 1 (n = 1662 from 17 hospitals) with prognostic estimation for the rapid stratification of PCR confirmed COVID-19 patients. These models, validated on Cohort 2 (n = 700) and Cohort 3 (n = 662) constructed from 9 external hospitals, achieved satisfying performance for predicting ICU, MV and death of COVID-19 patients (AUROC 0.916, 0.919 and 0.853), even on events happened two days later after admission (AUROC 0.919, 0.943 and 0.856). Both clinical and image features showed complementary roles in events prediction and provided accurate estimates to the time of progression (p<.001). Our findings are valuable for delivering timely treatment and optimizing the use of medical resources in the pandemic of COVID-19.</jats:p>
title: CT-based Rapid Triage of COVID-19 Patients: Risk Prediction and Progression Estimation of ICU Admission, Mechanical Ventilation, and Death of Hospitalized Patients
